info.title: Java Auth Template API
info.description:
Production-ready authentication and authorization REST API built with Spring Boot and stateless JWT.

## Authentication Flows

**JWT Bearer**: Obtain a token via `POST /api/v1/auth/login`, then include it in the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header on protected requests.

**OAuth2 Google**: Redirect-based flow. Initiate via `/oauth2/authorization/google`. After successful authentication, the JWT access token is returned in the redirect URL parameter.

**Token Expiry**: Access tokens are short-lived (5–15 minutes). When a token expires, the client receives HTTP 401 and must re-authenticate via `POST /api/v1/auth/login`. No refresh token mechanism exists — re-authentication is always explicit.

## Authorization Model

The system uses granular RBAC: users hold roles, roles carry permissions. All protected endpoints enforce permission checks server-side via Spring Security method-level authorization (`@PreAuthorize`). The required authority string must be present in the JWT `authorities` claim.

tags.name: Roles
tags.description: Role management and role-permission assignment. Requires `role:create`, `role:update`, or `role:delete` authority.

tags.name: User Role Assignment
tags.description: Assign and revoke roles on individual user accounts. Requires `role:update` authority.

tags.name: Permissions
tags.description: Permission catalog management. Follows the `resource:action` naming convention.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Registration, login, logout, password management, and email verification flows

tags.name: User Permission Assignment
tags.description: Grant and revoke direct permissions on individual user accounts, bypassing role-level assignment. Requires `permission:grant` or `permission:revoke` authority.

tags.name: Admin Security
tags.description: Administrative operations for credential invalidation and account lockout management. All endpoints require `auth:manage` authority.

tags.name: Audit
tags.description: Read-only access to the security audit event log and summary statistics. Requires `audit:view` authority.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: User profile management, admin user operations, and consent history

put.description: Transitions the user to INACTIVE and invalidates all previously issued JWTs. Requires `user:update` permission.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Optional reason for disabling the account

204.description: Account disabled; previously issued JWTs invalidated

put.description: Transitions the user from INACTIVE to ACTIVE. Requires `user:update` permission.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's non-sensitive profile data.

put.description: Updates permitted profile fields for the authenticated user.

get.description: Returns a single role by its UUID. Requires `role:create` or `role:update` authority.

put.description: Updates the description of an existing role. System roles cannot be renamed. Requires `role:update` authority.

delete.description: Removes a role. System roles and roles currently assigned to users cannot be deleted. Requires `role:delete` authority.

400.description: Role is in use or is a system role

get.description: Returns a paginated list of users. Requires `user:read` permission.

post.description: Creates a new user account on behalf of an admin. Requires `user:create` permission.
Grants a role to the specified user. Idempotent — assigning a role the user already holds is a no-op. Requires `role:update` authority.
Grants a direct permission to the specified user, independent of role assignments. Idempotent. Requires `permission:grant` authority.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all roles. Requires `role:create` or `role:update` authority.

post.description: Creates a new named role. Requires `role:create` authority.

get.description: Returns all permissions assigned to the given role. Requires `role:create` or `role:update` authority.

post.description: Grants a permission to a role. Idempotent — assigning an already-granted permission is a no-op. Requires `permission:grant` authority.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all registered permissions. Requires `permission:grant` or `audit:view` authority.

post.description: Registers a new named permission in the system catalog. Requires `permission:grant` authority.
Creates a new user account in PENDING_VERIFICATION status. Dispatches a verification email. Anti-enumeration: response is identical whether or not the email already exists.

400.description: Validation error (invalid email, weak password, missing consent)

post.description: Changes the authenticated user's password. Requires the current password for verification. Updates `credentials_updated_at`, invalidating all previously issued JWTs.

204.description: Password changed; previously issued JWTs invalidated

400.description: Validation error or wrong current password

post.description: Initiates a password reset flow for the given email. Anti-enumeration: always returns HTTP 200 regardless of whether the email exists.

200.description: Reset email dispatched if the address exists and is active

post.description: Validates the reset token and sets a new password. Updates `credentials_updated_at`, invalidating all previously issued JWTs. Token is single-use and expires after the configured TTL.
Records the AUTH_LOGOUT audit event. The client is responsible for discarding the JWT locally. No server-side session state is modified — the system is fully stateless.

Authenticates with email and password. Returns a short-lived JWT access token on success. Any failure (wrong credentials, locked account, unverified account) produces a generic HTTP 401. Response includes `Cache-Control: no-store`.

200.description: Authentication successful; JWT access token returned

401.description: Invalid credentials, locked or unverified account

post.description: Resends the email verification link for accounts in PENDING_VERIFICATION status. Anti-enumeration: always returns HTTP 200.
Dispatches a verification email to the new address. The current email remains active until the new one is verified.

200.description: Verification email dispatched to new address

post.description: Clears any active administrative or brute-force lock on the target user account. Requires `auth:manage` authority.
Applies an administrative lock to the target user account, preventing authentication regardless of brute-force counters. Requires `auth:manage` authority.
Updates `credentials_updated_at` for the target user, immediately invalidating all previously issued JWTs. The user must re-authenticate to obtain a new token. Requires `auth:manage` authority.

204.description: Re-authentication forced; all prior JWTs invalidated

get.description: Returns full user details for an admin lookup. Requires `user:read` permission.

delete.description: Sets `deleted_at` on the user record. No physical row removal. All previously issued JWTs are invalidated. Requires `user:delete` permission.

204.description: Account soft-deleted; previously issued JWTs invalidated

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's data-processing consent records.
Returns the authenticated user's non-sensitive profile data. Never returns password hash, raw tokens, or full IP history.
Activates the account by validating the email verification token. Transitions account from PENDING_VERIFICATION to ACTIVE.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Email verification token from the verification email

get.description: Returns a paginated, filtered list of audit events. Supports filtering by event type, actor, target user, outcome, and time range. Read-only. Requires `audit:view` authority.
Returns a chronological audit timeline for a specific user. Includes all events where the user was the actor or target. Read-only. Requires `audit:view` authority.
Returns aggregate security statistics: recent failure counts, locked accounts, and activity trends. Read-only. Requires `audit:view` authority.

delete.description: Removes a role from the specified user. Idempotent — revoking a role the user does not hold is a no-op. Requires `role:update` authority.
Removes a direct permission from the specified user. Idempotent. Requires `permission:revoke` authority.
Removes a permission from a role. Idempotent — revoking a permission not held is a no-op. Requires `permission:revoke` authority.
Removes a permission from the catalog. Permissions currently assigned to roles or users cannot be deleted. Requires `permission:revoke` authority.
Removes the linked OAuth2 provider from the account. Blocked if the account has no local password set (prevents account lockout).

400.description: Cannot unlink — no local password set

404.description: Provider not linked to this account

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error response envelope returned on all non-2xx responses

fieldErrors.description: Field-level validation errors; only present on HTTP 400 validation failures

bearerAuth.description: JWT access token. Obtain via `POST /api/v1/auth/login`. Include as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on authenticated requests.
bearerAuth.name: bearerAuth
